items.title: Bank of America Is the Big-Bank Holdout After This Year's Stress Tests. Here's What Its Next Move Could Look Like.
items.summary: Bank of America passed the Federal Reserve's stress tests but, unlike peers Goldman Sachs and Citigroup, did not immediately announce a dividend increase. The article suggests this is likely a timing issue, as BAC typically announces dividend increases in Q3 alongside earnings. With recent annual increases of 8% and 7%, another hike is expected soon. BAC trades at lower valuations than JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs, positioning it as a value play with a 1.92% dividend yield.

items.title: 3 Big Banks Plan Double Digit Dividend Increases After Passing Fed Stress Test
items.summary: Goldman Sachs, Wells Fargo, and Citigroup all passed the Federal Reserve's 2026 stress tests and announced double-digit dividend increases. Goldman plans to raise its dividend from $4.50 to $5 per share, Wells Fargo from 45 cents to 50 cents per share (11% increase), and Citigroup from 60 cents to 67 cents per share (12% increase). All three banks maintained capital ratios well above the 4.5% minimum requirement, demonstrating financial resilience.

items.title: Goldman Sachs Raised Its Dividend as Investment Banking Fees Rebound. Is the Stock a Buy?
items.summary: Goldman Sachs raised its quarterly dividend by 11% to $5.00 per share after passing the Federal Reserve's stress test, marking the 15th consecutive year of dividend increases. The bank is benefiting from a hot M&A market, with Q1 2026 seeing $1.2 trillion in deals and investment banking revenue up 48%. Goldman Sachs is positioned to earn significant fees from major upcoming IPOs including SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic, with the stock trading at 18 times forward earnings.

items.title: America's Biggest Banks Passed Their Stress Tests. Now They're Showering Investors With Cash.
items.summary: All 32 major U.S. banks passed the Federal Reserve's 2026 stress test, maintaining capital levels even under severe recession scenarios. JPMorgan Chase announced a $50 billion buyback and 10% dividend increase, while Morgan Stanley delivered the largest dividend increase at 15%. Goldman Sachs raised dividends 11%, and Wells Fargo increased dividends 11%. The announcements reflect strong confidence in bank stability, with Wells Fargo standing out as the cheapest valuation with the highest dividend yield.

items.title: This Nvidia-Backed Company Just Entered the $154 Billion AI Photonics Market. Does This Make the $13 Stock a No-Brainer Buy?
items.summary: Nokia is expanding its role in AI infrastructure through silicon photonics and optical networking solutions. The company announced expansion of photonic chip operations in Pennsylvania and reported strong Q1 2026 results with 49% YoY growth in its AI & Cloud segment and $1.1 billion in new AI infrastructure orders. Goldman Sachs projects the optical networking market tied to AI will grow from $15 billion in 2026 to $154 billion by 2028, with co-packaged optics representing a $91 billion opportunity. Recent insider buying by Nokia executives, combined with Nvidia's $1 billion strategic investment, suggests the stock has further upside potential.

items.title: A Blood Test That Screens for 50+ Cancers Just Moved Closer to Real-World Use. Here's What It Means for Grail Investors.
items.summary: Grail's Galleri multi-cancer early detection test missed its primary endpoint in a landmark NHS trial but still has a pathway to FDA approval and insurer coverage. Management points to favorable trends in detecting 12 deadly cancers, upcoming 12-month follow-up data, and strong positive predictive values as reasons for optimism. The stock's future depends on FDA decisions and insurer cost-benefit analyses.

items.title: Innovator ETFs® Announces Liquidation of Two Exchange-Traded Funds
items.summary: Innovator Capital Management announced that its Board of Trustees has approved liquidation plans for the Innovator Gradient Tactical Rotation Strategy ETF and Innovator Hedged Nasdaq-100 ETF, effective June 17, 2026. Shareholders can trade shares until July 10, 2026, with liquidation expected around July 15, 2026. The announcement comes after Goldman Sachs acquired Innovator on April 1, 2026.

items.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Lands Lead Role On SpaceX IPO As Dealmaking Outlook Brightens - Sahm
items.summary: Goldman Sachs (GS) Lands Lead Role On SpaceX IPO As Dealmaking Outlook Brightens    Sahm

items.title: Why Signet Jewelers Stock Triumphed on Tuesday
items.summary: Signet Jewelers stock rose 2.59% after announcing a $50 million accelerated share repurchase (ASR) program with Goldman Sachs. The program will deliver approximately 480,000 shares and should be completed between June 12 and July 17. Signet has over $355 million remaining in buyback authorizations and maintains over $600 million in cash.

items.title: SpaceX and Anthropic Fuel the Biggest IPO Boom on Record
items.summary: SpaceX and Anthropic are leading a record IPO boom in 2026. SpaceX begins trading Friday, while Anthropic's Claude AI is expected to drive even greater success. The IPO surge will boost underwriting revenue for firms like Goldman Sachs and support overall market confidence. Recent AI stock corrections are viewed as healthy profit-taking and rotation, with market breadth expanding amid strong economic growth indicators.

items.title: Why Peloton Stock Zoomed More Than 17% Higher Last Month
items.summary: Peloton Interactive's stock surged over 17% in May 2026 following its return to profitability in Q3 FY2026, posting $26 million in net income versus a $48 million loss year-over-year. The company achieved modest 1% revenue growth to $631 million and raised full-year guidance. Additional support came from inclusion in the S&P SmallCap 600 index. However, connected fitness subscriptions declined nearly 8% to 2.66 million, raising concerns about business sustainability.

items.title: Dip Buying Prevails as Semiconductor Selloff Fails to Derail the AI Boom
items.summary: Semiconductor stocks initially plunged 5.2% following disappointing guidance from Broadcom and CrowdStrike, but dip buyers quickly stepped in, limiting losses to 3.25% within the first hour. The broader market showed resilience with the Dow up 1.61%, driven by falling crude oil prices and interest rate declines amid Iran ceasefire rumors. Financial services and healthcare sectors led gains, while the AI boom remained intact despite the semiconductor weakness.

items.title: Jamie Dimon Hits the Road as SpaceX IPO Machine Roars to Life
items.summary: SpaceX's upcoming IPO, valued at approximately $1.8 trillion with an expected $75 billion raise, is shaping up as the largest IPO in market history. JPMorgan is conducting a nationwide client roadshow with CEO Jamie Dimon to drive demand beyond institutional investors into private wealth channels. The IPO is being positioned as a test of investor appetite for massive growth stories, with potential to reopen the IPO window for other AI and technology giants like OpenAI and Anthropic if successful.
